22 Orthobullets question mapping(s) for trauma-clavicle-fractures-midshaft (metadata only — no stems/answers)

metadata.description: Nonoperative sling support for acceptable clavicle fracture alignment.

metadata.claim_text: Posterior sternoclavicular displacement can threaten mediastinal structures and is not a benign clavicle injury.
Clavicle nonunion can present with persistent pain, motion limitation, or hardware issues after failed healing.
Treating every displaced midshaft clavicle fracture as mandatory ORIF without discussing nonunion risk and patient goals.

metadata.action: Discuss clavicle ORIF with attending; document nonoperative risks and patient goals
metadata.trigger: Displaced midshaft clavicle fracture with shortening, comminution, or high functional demand

metadata.description: Operative plate fixation for selected displaced clavicle fractures.
Failure of clavicle fracture healing with persistent pain or instability.

metadata.claim_text: Midshaft clavicle fractures are the most common clavicle fracture location after shoulder-directed trauma.
Describe fracture location, shortening, skin tenting, neurovascular exam, and sling versus operative plan with follow-up.

metadata.action: Sling immobilization with pain control and gradual return of motion when healing permits
metadata.trigger: Acceptable alignment in patient suitable for sling immobilization

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Midshaft Clavicle Fracture Pattern

metadata.description: Typical middle-third clavicle fracture displacement pattern.
Healed clavicle deformity affecting shoulder contour or function.
Fracture of the clavicle, commonly midshaft in traumatic shoulder girdle injuries.
